visual_role: Show Anthropic's behavior-training grammar from principles through critique, revision, AI feedback, and deployment caveats.
caption: Figure 12.1 - Constitutional AI As Behavior Grammar. Written principles, critique/revision, AI feedback, and evaluation loops can shape assistant behavior, but they do not prove solved alignment or moral guarantees.
blocked_claims: Solved alignment; moral guarantee; replacement for human judgment; deployed safety proof.

visual_role: Show Claude as a tiered/product family from Claude 3 through Claude 4 without converting launch posts into leaderboard proof.
caption: Figure 12.2 - Claude Becomes A Product Family. Haiku, Sonnet, Opus, computer use, Claude Code, and Claude 4 mark release surfaces and product positioning, not permanent best-model status.
blocked_claims: Exact benchmark superiority; live rank; pricing comparison; adoption; customer productivity.

figure_title: Computer Use, MCP, And Claude Code Bridge
visual_role: Show the bridge from chat to action through UI use, connectors, repository context, permissions, tools, and human review.
caption: Figure 12.3 - From Assistant To Action Surface. Anthropic's computer-use, MCP, and Claude Code materials show a move toward tool-connected assistants, with permissions and review as part of the system.
blocked_claims: General autonomy; MCP dominance; secure-by-default agents; productivity; adoption; reliable real-world control.

visual_role: Make tempting Anthropic/Claude claims visibly conditional: safety, benchmarks, pricing, productivity, adoption, and memory each need separate evidence.
caption: Figure 12.4 - The Tempting Claude Claims Are Not The Sourced Ones. Vendor launch pages and docs can show product direction, but outcome claims need benchmark, pricing, adoption, productivity, and safety evidence at the same scope.
blocked_claims: Best coding model; exact price-quality; productivity lift; enterprise adoption; solved safety; general memory; replacement.
